strengths: Nearly two decades of engineering experience with recent hands-on agentic-AI infrastructure work at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows — directly relevant to the role's agentic architecture mandate

Proven distributed-systems depth: low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) using async I/O, caching and real-time data, plus an event-driven, Kubernetes-deployable platform he founded and still operates

Fintech and commercial-platform exposure — investment-platform engineering at RIDE Capital and pricing optimization at Optilyz — combined with strong engineering-discipline credentials (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and a long record of mentoring, workshops and technical community leadership
concerns: No prior CTO or executive title; leadership evidence is craft mentorship and workshops rather than a documented record of scaling an engineering org from 10 to 30+ or building a senior leadership layer
Agentic-AI experience is recent (April–October 2025) and framed as tooling and infrastructure contribution rather than owning production multi-agent systems with financial consequences
No stated experience governing outsourced/hybrid engineering models or board-level roadmap communication, and the resume shows a current career gap since October 2025

key_factors.factor: Hands-on agentic-AI and MCP infrastructure experience
Deep distributed-systems and real-time backend expertise
Fintech-domain exposure at RIDE Capital and Optilyz
Absence of demonstrated executive-level org-scaling track record

response.justification: Bogdan Szabo presents a credible technical foundation for this role's hardest requirements: he has genuinely rare low-latency distributed-systems experience at massive request volumes, current hands-on work building agentic-AI infrastructure and MCP tooling, and a decade-long track record designing event-driven platforms he personally architected, deployed and operated. His fintech exposure at RIDE Capital and pricing-systems work at Optilyz map to the domain, and his sustained investment in TDD, CI/CD, testing frameworks and developer education signals exactly the evaluation-rigor and production-reliability culture the role demands. Read favourably, he is a deeply technical builder whose recent agentic pivot positions him to design the multi-agent architecture himself rather than delegate it, making him a strong advance-to-interview candidate on technical substance, with executive scope the open question to probe.
